# what if… economics

Interactive economics simulator. Adjust one macroeconomic factor and watch the
change ripple through related factors. Free, no login, no prior knowledge needed.
Available in English and Japanese (language switches client-side on one URL).

## How it works
- Single-page static web app; requires JavaScript.
- Pick a factor, change it, and the app shows the directional knock-on effects on
  related factors, with notes on caveats and conditions.

## Data
- Factor relationships and scenarios are stored as static JSON under /data.
- Directional factor relationships (how one factor affects another) are a small
  machine-readable knowledge graph:
  https://whatifeconomics.com/data/relationships.json - agents can answer "how
  does X affect Y" from this directly.
- Copy is localised via /i18n (ui.en.json, ui.ja.json).
- The data models directional, illustrative relationships for learning; it is not
  a forecast or a citable dataset.

## Links
- App: https://whatifeconomics.com/
- Relationships data: https://whatifeconomics.com/data/relationships.json
